mnemonics: MAMA: Matrices Are Multi-dimensional Arrays (but only 2D)
ROCA: Row, Order, Column, Arrangement (for matrix(data, nrow, ncol, byrow))
keyPoints: A matrix is a 2D array of elements of the same data type
Matrices are created using the matrix() function with data, nrow, and ncol arguments
Arrays can have more than two dimensions, unlike matrices which are strictly 2D
commonMistakes: Mixing up row and column indices when subsetting (e.g., my_matrix[2,1] vs my_matrix[1,2])
Forgetting that matrix operations require compatible dimensions
Attempting to store multiple data types in a matrix, which coerces all elements to a single type
